text.title: growth-onboarding-emails Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for growth-onboarding-emails as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Sending more than one email per day during onboarding.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Frequency above 1/day causes unsubscribe spikes that hurt overall deliverability, not just the onboarding sequence. Deliverability damage is disproportionate to the marginal activation gain.

failure-mode.description: Including multiple CTAs in a single onboarding email (e.g., "Complete setup" AND "Explore features" AND "Invite a teammate").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multiple CTAs split user attention. The user chooses nothing or the wrong thing. One email, one action.

failure-mode.description: Using only time-based triggers (Day 1, Day 3, Day 7) with no behavioral conditions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Time-based emails reach activated and inactive users identically. An activated user receives a "still getting started?" email after completing setup — undermining trust.

failure-mode.description: Using generic copy not tied to segment or trigger context (e.g., "Here are some tips!" to all users regardless of their activity).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Generic copy signals the email was not written for the recipient. Personalization based on actual behavior (even "I see you created a project") doubles reply rate.

failure-mode.description: Skipping the escalation path — no emails for users who take no action after Day 2.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Users who get stuck silently churn. Without escalation, the sequence abandons users at the exact moment they need help most.

failure-mode.description: Using personalization tokens without verifying the ESP's event tracking is reliably firing them (e.g., "You created your first project!" when the event is not reliably tracked).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Broken personalization is worse than no personalization — it signals a broken product or broken communication system.

failure-mode.description: Automating the Day 10 personal outreach email at full scale without a human review step.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Personal outreach emails feel authentic only if they can be followed up. A fully automated "book a call" email with no human behind it damages trust when the user tries to reply.
